Page 10, Remark 2.6 (end of Section 2.2).

Certainly, to make this method fully rigorous, it remains to show the theoretic convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration, which is a nontrivial problem in itself and will be left for future research.
title: Convergence of single-valued fixed-point iteration for Gibbs-form equilibrium operators
background_latex:
Consider a discrete-time infinite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with finite state space \(S=\{1,\dots,d\}\) and compact action space \(U\subset \mathbb{R}^\ell\) with \(0<\mathrm{Leb}(U)<\infty\). A relaxed (randomized) feedback control is a map \(\pi:S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\), where \(\mathcal{P}(U)\) denotes probability measures on \(U\). For \(\lambda>0\), an entropy-regularized objective is used in which the stage payoff includes a Shannon differential entropy term. Under entropy regularization, the equilibrium (in the sense of intra-personal subgame-perfect equilibrium for time-inconsistent discounting) can be characterized as a fixed point of a single-valued operator of Gibbs (softmax) form.

More concretely, for each state \(i\in S\) and vector \(y\in\mathbb{R}^d\), define a probability density \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)\in \mathcal{D}(U)\) by
\[\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)(u):=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,u)+p_i^u\cdot y)\right)}{\int_U \exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,v)+p_i^v\cdot y)\right)\,dv},\qquad u\in U,\]
where \(f(0,i,u)\) is the one-step reward (at time-difference \(0\)) and \(p_i^u\in\mathbb{R}^d\) is the transition-probability row vector from state \(i\) under action \(u\). Given \(y\), the corresponding relaxed control is \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot):S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\). Let \(J_\lambda^\pi\) be the entropy-regularized value functional and let \(V_\lambda^\pi\in\mathbb{R}^d\) be the associated continuation-value vector (expected future discounted reward plus discounted entropy starting one step ahead). Define the induced single-valued self-map \(\Psi_\lambda:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^d\) by
\[\Psi_\lambda(y):=V_\lambda^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}.\]
Fixed points \(y^\star\) of \(\Psi_\lambda\) correspond to entropy-regularized relaxed equilibria \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\).

A natural numerical approach is fixed-point iteration on \(\Psi_\lambda\) (equivalently on the policy operator \(\Phi_\lambda(\pi):=\Gamma_\lambda(V_\lambda^\pi,\cdot)\)), i.e., start from some \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) (or \(\pi_0\)) and generate \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Give conditions under which the single-valued fixed-point iteration
\[y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\qquad (n\ge 0)
\]
converges (e.g., from arbitrary initialization, or from some specified set of initial points) to a fixed point \(y^\star\in\mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying \(\Psi_\lambda(y^\star)=y^\star\), equivalently producing a regular relaxed equilibrium \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\) for the entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP.
latex:
Consider a discrete-time infinite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with finite state space \(S=\{1,\dots,d\}\) and compact action space \(U\subset \mathbb{R}^\ell\) with \(0<\mathrm{Leb}(U)<\infty\). A relaxed (randomized) feedback control is a map \(\pi:S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\), where \(\mathcal{P}(U)\) denotes probability measures on \(U\). For \(\lambda>0\), an entropy-regularized objective is used in which the stage payoff includes a Shannon differential entropy term. Under entropy regularization, the equilibrium (in the sense of intra-personal subgame-perfect equilibrium for time-inconsistent discounting) can be characterized as a fixed point of a single-valued operator of Gibbs (softmax) form.

More concretely, for each state \(i\in S\) and vector \(y\in\mathbb{R}^d\), define a probability density \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)\in \mathcal{D}(U)\) by
\[\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)(u):=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,u)+p_i^u\cdot y)\right)}{\int_U \exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,v)+p_i^v\cdot y)\right)\,dv},\qquad u\in U,\]
where \(f(0,i,u)\) is the one-step reward (at time-difference \(0\)) and \(p_i^u\in\mathbb{R}^d\) is the transition-probability row vector from state \(i\) under action \(u\). Given \(y\), the corresponding relaxed control is \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot):S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\). Let \(J_\lambda^\pi\) be the entropy-regularized value functional and let \(V_\lambda^\pi\in\mathbb{R}^d\) be the associated continuation-value vector (expected future discounted reward plus discounted entropy starting one step ahead). Define the induced single-valued self-map \(\Psi_\lambda:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^d\) by
\[\Psi_\lambda(y):=V_\lambda^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}.\]
Fixed points \(y^\star\) of \(\Psi_\lambda\) correspond to entropy-regularized relaxed equilibria \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\).

A natural numerical approach is fixed-point iteration on \(\Psi_\lambda\) (equivalently on the policy operator \(\Phi_\lambda(\pi):=\Gamma_\lambda(V_\lambda^\pi,\cdot)\)), i.e., start from some \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) (or \(\pi_0\)) and generate \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)).

Give conditions under which the single-valued fixed-point iteration
\[y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\qquad (n\ge 0)
\]
converges (e.g., from arbitrary initialization, or from some specified set of initial points) to a fixed point \(y^\star\in\mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying \(\Psi_\lambda(y^\star)=y^\star\), equivalently producing a regular relaxed equilibrium \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\) for the entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP.
open_problem_quote: Certainly, to make this method fully rigorous, it remains to show the theoretic convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration, which is a nontrivial problem in itself and will be left for future research.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally test convergence or non-convergence of the iteration on many randomly generated finite-state instances by explicitly evaluating \(\Psi_\lambda\) and iterating, but the space of instances is large and failure modes may be sensitive to parameter choices and initialization.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate updates (damping, averaging, alternative operators, or sufficient-condition discovery) can be represented as code and scored by an automatic fitness signal such as empirical convergence rate or basin of attraction over a distribution of MDP instances, making evolutionary search plausible for discovering robustly convergent schemes or sharp conjectures.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In Bayraktar–Huang–Wang–Zhou (2024), entropy regularization turns the equilibrium best-response into a single-valued Gibbs map \(\Gamma_\lambda\), yielding a single-valued equilibrium operator \(\Psi_\lambda(y)=V^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}_\lambda\). Existence of fixed points is obtained via Brouwer after establishing uniform logarithmic entropy bounds (Lemma 2.1) under Lipschitz dependence on actions and a uniform cone condition on \(U\). The paper explicitly leaves open the convergence of the naive fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (Remark 2.6), which would make the approximation-by-regularization approach fully rigorous as a computational method.

Among forward citations, the closest progress on iteration convergence is in “Equilibrium for Time-inconsistent Mean Field Games: A Systematic Analysis by Entropy Regularization”, which proves contraction and convergence of a Gibbs-form policy-iteration map \(\Phi_\lambda\) under restrictive conditions (small horizon and weak terminal interaction) in a continuous-time finite-horizon mean-field setting. This suggests that proving convergence in the discrete-time infinite-horizon finite-state MDP likely requires additional structure ensuring that \(\Psi_\lambda\) (or \(\Phi_\lambda\)) is a contraction or at least has a globally attracting fixed point—e.g., strong discounting/mixing, small effective horizon \(\sum_t \delta(t)\), or quantitative Lipschitz bounds on \(y\mapsto V^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}_\lambda\) combined with the \(1/\lambda\)-Lipschitz nature of the softmax map.

Other citing works mainly develop existence/approximation theories via entropy regularization and Schauder/Kakutani fixed points (including mean-field stopping and diffusion-control analogues) but do not establish general convergence of the fixed-point iteration for the Gibbs operator. Therefore, based on the provided forward-citation set, the convergence problem for single-valued fixed-point iteration of \(\Psi_\lambda\) in the Bayraktar–Huang–Wang–Zhou discrete-time infinite-horizon setting remains open.

sources.title: Equilibrium for Time-inconsistent Mean Field Games: A Systematic Analysis by Entropy Regularization
sources.theorem_or_result: In a finite-horizon continuous-time time-inconsistent mean field game with compact action set and Shannon entropy regularization, the paper proves: - Existence of a regularized relaxed mean-field equilibrium \((\pi^*_\lambda,m^*_\lambda)\) for all sufficiently small \(\lambda>0\) via a Schauder fixed point for an operator \(\Phi_\lambda\) built from the Gibbs map \(\Gamma_\lambda\) and the induced value/measure flow (Theorem 3.1(ii)). - As \(\lambda\to 0\), along a subsequence \((\pi_{\lambda_n},m_{\lambda_n})\to(\pi^\infty,m^\infty)\) yielding an equilibrium of the unregularized time-inconsistent MFG (Theorem 4.1). - For fixed \(\lambda>0\), a policy-iteration algorithm \((J^{k+1},m^{k+1})=\Phi_\lambda(J^k,m^k)\) converges to the regularized equilibrium provided the horizon \(T\) is small and terminal interaction is weak (Theorem 5.1).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for conditions ensuring convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (equivalently policy iteration \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)) for entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDPs with Gibbs-form equilibrium operator. This paper proves a convergence result for a closely related fixed-point/policy-iteration map \(\Phi_\lambda\) in a different setting: continuous-time finite-horizon time-inconsistent mean field games. Under additional assumptions (notably short time horizon and weak terminal measure dependence), they show \(\Phi_\lambda\) is a strict contraction and policy iteration converges to the unique regularized equilibrium (Theorem 5.1). This provides partial progress and transferable techniques (contraction estimates using Bismut–Elworthy–Li gradient bounds and stability of measure flows), but it does not directly resolve the discrete-time infinite-horizon finite-state MDP iteration convergence question, nor does it give global-from-arbitrary-initialization convergence without small-time/weak-interaction restrictions.

sources.title: Time-inconsistent mean-field stopping problems: a regularised equilibrium approach
sources.theorem_or_result: For a time-inconsistent discrete-time mean-field stopping MDP with entropy regularization, the paper defines a Gibbs/logit best-response operator \(T_1^\lambda\) and a continuation-value operator \(T_2^\lambda\), and proves existence of a regularized equilibrium \(\varphi_\lambda\) as a fixed point \(\varphi_\lambda = T_1^\lambda\circ T_2^\lambda(\varphi_\lambda)\) (Theorem 3.2, Corollary 3.3). It further proves that along a sequence \(\lambda_n\downarrow 0\), \(\varphi_{\lambda_n}\) converges (in a weak-* sense, upgraded after one-step transition) to a relaxed equilibrium \(\varphi_0\) of the unregularized problem (Theorem 3.5).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for conditions ensuring convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or policy iteration \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)) for Gibbs-form equilibrium operators in entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDPs. This paper also uses entropy regularization to obtain a single-valued Gibbs/logit equilibrium operator and proves existence of fixed points via Schauder, but it does not establish convergence of the naive fixed-point iteration from arbitrary initialization. It proposes a policy-iteration-style numerical scheme (Algorithm 1) for a specific mean-field stopping model and provides illustrative numerical convergence, yet without a general contraction/monotonicity argument guaranteeing convergence of \(\varphi^{(n+1)}=T_1^\lambda\circ T_2^\lambda(\varphi^{(n)})\) or the value-based iteration. Thus it provides related existence/compactness tools and an operator framework closely aligned with the open problem, but not the requested convergence theory for fixed-point iteration.

sources.title: Equilibrium under time-inconsistency: A new existence theory by vanishing entropy regularization
sources.theorem_or_result:
In a continuous-time time-inconsistent diffusion control model with compact action space and Shannon entropy regularization, the paper proves:
- (Existence for fixed
sources.connection: The open problem is about discrete-time finite-state MDPs and asks for conditions ensuring convergence of the single-valued Gibbs-form fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\). This paper does not analyze that iteration or provide contraction/monotonicity conditions for \(\Psi_\lambda\) in the discrete-time setting. Instead it studies continuous-time diffusion models and uses Schauder fixed points to prove existence of entropy-regularized equilibria and then proves convergence of regularized equilibria as \(\lambda\to 0\). The techniques (Gibbs-form policy operator, compactness/Schauder fixed point, a priori estimates) are related tools, but they do not establish convergence of the fixed-point iteration itself.

sources.title: Existence of Markov equilibrium control in discrete time
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper proves an existence theorem for discrete-time finite-horizon time-inconsistent control: under Assumption 3.3 (nonnegative Borel costs; stage costs inf-compact in controls; monotone l.s.c. nonlinearity; and a strong continuity condition on transition kernels that makes \(u\mapsto\int V(x')P_t(dx'\mid x,u)\) continuous for every bounded Borel \(V\)), there exists a nonrandomized Borel measurable Markov equilibrium policy \(\pi^*=(u_1^*,\dots,u_{T-1}^*)\) (Theorem 3.6).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for conditions ensuring convergence of a single-valued fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) for an entropy-regularized infinite-horizon MDP, where \(\Psi_\lambda\) is a Gibbs/softmax equilibrium operator. This citing paper does not study entropy regularization, Gibbs-form operators, infinite horizon, or convergence of fixed-point iterations. Its contribution is instead an existence result for finite-horizon equilibria via measurable selection and inf-compactness. Indirectly, it provides background tools (inf-compactness, strong continuity of kernels) that can help establish existence/regularity of equilibrium selectors, which is a prerequisite for analyzing iterative schemes, but it does not provide contraction/monotonicity/nonexpansiveness properties of \(\Psi_\lambda\) or any convergence theorem for iterating it.

sources.title: Existence of equilibria for time-inconsistent games in discrete time
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper introduces and analyzes a new equilibrium notion for discrete-time infinite-horizon time-inconsistent finite-state mean field games: a (time-homogeneous, distribution-feedback) policy \(	ilde\pi:\Delta=[d]	imes\mathcal P([d])	o\mathcal P(U)\) is a consistent MFG equilibrium if for all \((x,
u)\in\Delta\),
\[J_{	ilde\pi}(x,
u)=\sup_{\varpi\in\mathcal P(U)} J_{\varpi\otimes_1 	ilde\pi}(x,
u,\mu^{	ilde\pi,
u}_{[1:\infty)})	ag{4.3}\]
(equivalently \(	ilde\pi\) is a fixed point of a best-response correspondence \(\Gamma\), Corollary 4.2). Assuming Lipschitz regularity (Assumption 4.1) and continuity of \(	ilde\pi\) in \(
u\), Theorem 4.1 shows that the replicating \(N\)-tuple \((	ilde\pi,\dots,	ilde\pi)\) is a consistent \(\varepsilon\)-equilibrium for the \(N\)-agent game for all large \(N\). Theorem 4.2 gives a convergence result: if \(	ilde\pi_N	o	ilde\pi_\infty\) uniformly in \(W_1\) and each \((	ilde\pi_N,\dots,	ilde\pi_N)\) is a consistent equilibrium for the \(N\)-agent game, then \(	ilde\pi_\infty\) is a consistent MFG equilibrium.
sources.connection: The open problem asks for conditions guaranteeing convergence of a single-valued Gibbs/softmax fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) for entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP equilibria. This citing paper does not study entropy regularization, Gibbs-form operators, or convergence of a single-valued fixed-point iteration. Instead it develops equilibrium concepts for time-inconsistent mean field games and proves existence of classic equilibria via Kakutani-Fan (set-valued best-response) and analyzes a different fixed-point characterization \(\tilde\pi\in\Gamma(\tilde\pi)\) (set-valued) for “consistent equilibria,” plus mean-field limit results. These fixed-point/existence techniques and continuity/Lipschitz assumptions are conceptually related tools, but they do not provide contraction/monotonicity-type conditions or iteration convergence results for the Gibbs operator \(\Psi_\lambda\) in the original MDP setting.
